prerequisites: Check available disk space: dir bootflash: (NX-OS/IOS-XE) or show media (IOS-XR)
Backup running configuration to external server
Verify new image integrity (MD5/SHA512 checksum against cisco.com)
Review release notes for target version — check for known caveats and ISSU support
Confirm hardware compatibility (image must support installed line cards / supervisors)
Schedule maintenance window with change control approval
Ensure console access is available (not just SSH/VTY)
For dual-sup systems: verify standby sup is online and in sync
purpose: Upgrade, downgrade, or patch the OS image on a network device [iosxe — method: install mode (add/activate/commit); platforms: Catalyst 9000, Catalyst 8000, ASR 1000, ISR 4000]

rollback.action: install rollback to <point> — rolls back to a previous install point
If auto-abort-timer was set and commit not done: device auto-rolls back on reload
Bundle mode fallback: boot system flash:<old_image>.bin, then reload from rommon if needed

steps.notes: Verify MD5: verify /md5 flash:<image>. Ensure sufficient space: dir flash:

steps.action: Set boot mode to install mode (if not already)
steps.notes: Install mode uses packages.conf. Bundle mode (boot system flash:<image>.bin) is legacy. Check current mode: show version | include Installation mode
Extracts packages from the bundle. Does NOT activate yet.
Activates staged packages. Will prompt for reload confirmation. Add 'auto-abort-timer <min>' for automatic rollback if you don't commit within the timer.
CRITICAL: If you don't commit, the device rolls back to the previous version on next reload. Commit after verifying the new version is stable.

verification.confirms_if: Stack is healthy post-upgrade (stacking platforms only)
verification.look_for: All stack members show 'Ready' with same version
CPU and memory within normal range

warnings: ISSU is NOT supported on all platforms or between all version combinations — always verify the ISSU compatibility matrix
Always have console access before starting an upgrade — if the device fails to boot you need console to recover
Verify boot variables after upgrade to prevent boot loop on next reload
On dual-sup systems, upgrade BOTH supervisors — mismatched versions cause unpredictable behavior
SMU/GISO patches may have dependencies — install in the order specified by release notes
Never delete the currently running image until the new image is confirmed stable
On stacks: 'install activate' upgrades ALL stack members and reloads the entire stack
ISSU on IOS-XE requires StackWise Virtual or redundant sup — single-sup platforms always reload
Bundle mode (boot system flash:<image>.bin) is simpler but does not support rollback or SMU
After V-major upgrade (e.g., 16.x → 17.x), verify SDM template and feature compatibility
